hero.title: Find Your Dream House
hero.subtitle: Japan has over 9 million vacant houses (akiya). Many are available for free or at incredibly low prices. We find them, translate them, and connect you with local agents.

whyJapan.title: Why Buy Property in Japan?
whyJapan.noRestrictionsDesc: Japan is one of the few countries where foreigners can buy property with no restrictions — no visa, no residency required.
whyJapan.affordableDesc: Houses starting from $0 (free akiya programs). Average rural properties cost $3,000–$30,000. A fraction of Western prices.
whyJapan.architectureDesc: Traditional kominka, machiya townhouses, and countryside farmhouses offer character and charm found nowhere else in the world.

howItWorksSection.title: How AkiyaFinder Works
howItWorksSection.step1Desc: We search Japanese-only property databases, akiya banks, and SUUMO daily to find the best deals across Japan.
howItWorksSection.step2Desc: All property information, including location details, pricing, and features, is translated into clear English for you.
howItWorksSection.step3Desc: When you find a property you love, we connect you with a licensed Japanese real estate agent who speaks English.

aboutPage.title: About AkiyaFinder
aboutPage.intro: AkiyaFinder helps international buyers discover and invest in Japan's vacant properties. We translate akiya bank listings into English and Chinese, add investment metrics, and connect you with licensed local agents.
aboutPage.agentMatchingDesc: Free agent matching — no cost to buyers
aboutPage.whyP1: Japan has over 9 million vacant homes, and that number is growing every year. Many of these properties are available for incredibly low prices — some even for free. But there's a massive information gap: almost all akiya bank listings are in Japanese only, scattered across 1,700+ municipal websites with no standardized format.
aboutPage.whyP2: We bridge that gap by automatically collecting, translating, and enriching akiya listings with investment-relevant data — price per square meter, station access, building condition, Airbnb potential — so international buyers can make informed decisions.
aboutPage.step1Desc: Search properties on our investment map. Filter by price, location, station access, and investment category.
aboutPage.step2Desc: Submit an inquiry and we'll connect you with a licensed Japanese real estate agent who speaks English — within 2 business days.
aboutPage.step3Desc: Your agent handles everything — property visits, negotiation, contracts, and registration. Many purchases can be completed remotely.
aboutPage.freeForBuyers: Free for buyers — We never charge buyers for agent introductions
aboutPage.licensedOnly: Licensed agents only — Every agent we recommend holds a valid Japanese real estate license (宅建業免許)
aboutPage.noPressure: No pressure — Browse freely, inquire when ready. No obligations
aboutPage.updatedRegularly: Updated regularly — We refresh listings from akiya banks across Japan
aboutPage.readyDesc: Start browsing properties on our investment map.

howItWorksPage.title: How to Buy a House in Japan
howItWorksPage.subtitle: Buying property in Japan as a foreigner is simpler than you think. Here's everything you need to know.
howItWorksPage.canBuyTitle: Can Foreigners Buy Property in Japan?
howItWorksPage.canBuyYes: Yes! Japan is one of the few countries in the world with zero restrictions on foreign property ownership.
howItWorksPage.canBuySameRights: Same property rights as Japanese citizens
howItWorksPage.canBuyRemote: You can buy from overseas without visiting Japan
howItWorksPage.whatIsAkiyaP1: Akiya (空き家) literally means "empty house" in Japanese. Due to Japan's aging population and urban migration, there are over 9 million vacant houses across the country.
howItWorksPage.whatIsAkiyaP2: Many local governments offer these houses for free or at very low prices through Akiya Bank (空き家バンク) programs, sometimes with renovation subsidies of ¥1,000,000+ (~$6,600+).
howItWorksPage.whatIsAkiyaP3: The problem? Almost all akiya information is in Japanese only. That's where AkiyaFinder comes in.
howItWorksPage.step1Desc: Browse our curated listings, all translated into English. Filter by price, location, and property type.
howItWorksPage.step2Desc: Click 'Inquire' on any property. Tell us about yourself and what you're looking for. It's free and no obligation.
howItWorksPage.step3Title: Get Connected to a Local Agent
howItWorksPage.step3Desc: We'll connect you with a licensed, English-speaking real estate agent in that area. They'll handle everything from here.
howItWorksPage.step4Desc: Your agent arranges a viewing (in-person or virtual). They'll check the property condition, legal status, and any issues.
howItWorksPage.step5Desc: Your agent negotiates the price and handles all paperwork in Japanese. You'll receive English translations of key documents.
howItWorksPage.step6Desc: Pay the purchase price (bank transfer). The agent registers the property in your name. You now own a house in Japan!
howItWorksPage.costPropertyNote: Many akiya are free or under $10,000
howItWorksPage.faq1Q: Do I need to visit Japan to buy?
howItWorksPage.faq1A: Not necessarily. Many purchases can be completed remotely with a power of attorney. However, visiting is recommended to see the property condition firsthand.
howItWorksPage.faq2Q: Can I get a mortgage as a foreigner?
howItWorksPage.faq2A: Generally, non-resident foreigners cannot get Japanese mortgages. Most akiya purchases are cash transactions, which is feasible given the low prices ($0–$30,000 for many properties).
howItWorksPage.faq3A: The main additional costs are agent fees (~3%), registration taxes (~1-2%), and renovation costs if needed. Annual property tax is very low for rural properties (often under $300/year).
howItWorksPage.faq4Q: Why are houses so cheap in Japan?
howItWorksPage.faq4A: Japan's population is shrinking and concentrating in cities. Rural areas have surplus housing with no demand. Japanese houses also depreciate (unlike Western countries), so older houses approach zero value.
howItWorksPage.faq5Q: Is AkiyaFinder a real estate agency?
howItWorksPage.faq5A: No. We are an information service that curates and translates Japanese property listings. When you're ready to buy, we connect you with licensed Japanese real estate agents.

footer.aboutDesc: Japan's akiya property search platform for international investors.
footer.disclaimerText: AkiyaFinder is an information and referral service. We are not a licensed real estate agency. All property transactions are handled by licensed Japanese real estate professionals (宅建業者). Property information is for reference only.

yesSection.title: Can foreigners buy property in Japan?
yesSection.a1detail: Japanese law places no restrictions on foreign property ownership. Full ownership rights for any nationality.
yesSection.q2: Do I need a visa or residency?
yesSection.a2: Yes, you can buy — even on a tourist visa.
yesSection.a2detail: No permanent residency or work visa required. You can purchase property on a tourist visa with full ownership rights.
yesSection.q3: Can I buy without speaking Japanese?
yesSection.a3detail: AkiyaFinder provides full translation support — from property search to agent negotiation, all in English.
yesSection.a4detail: Japan has ~9 million vacant homes. Some municipalities offer them completely free through akiya bank programs.
yesSection.q5: Can I use it for investment (Airbnb, etc)?
yesSection.a5detail: Akiya properties can be used for living, vacation homes, Airbnb (with proper registration), renovation projects, and more.
